item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why I first thought of China…However later I thought it could be Spain or Italy or Greece or blahblahblah.I am really confused. Upvoted.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that this page has been tagged with "event-featured", but due to the current front page slots being occupied by Doomsday Contest winners for this month, the feature will take place next month. Sorry for any confusion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since the authorship is no longer clear from this: this skip was written by me, Tufto, on my first account prior to my deleting it. You can find more of my stuff here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Presumably because he doesn't actually have the power to do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She also could be the US, if the SCP was listing either Idaho, Texas, or Maine rather than the US itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The end of the conversation, bringing to the skip's attention the remainder of Tehrani's identity through his ignorance of early Persian foodstuffs was brilliant. I love the way this entire article was handled. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
some guy impersonating the Mahdi through anomalous means

You know, I'm not too sure of that, personally. Identity is a fickle thing. Plus, if he really is just an impersonator, why chill for a thousand years instead of lighting up the world already, as he clearly desired? I believe there is reasonable doubt as to the authenticity of the anomaly. Just my interpretation, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ordinarily, I don't like SCPs that are religious items or figures brought to life. However, given that this is pretty clearly some guy impersonating the Mahdi through anomalous means and not the actual Twelfth Imam, I think this is pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking Spain, but it's open to interpretation :p.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of course not. You come from █████, yes? A country that helped shape the world we inhabit, that built empires over boundless oceans, that created the very concepts of life within which we live and breathe today.

Italy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good — I really enjoy SCPs that play with religion in a way other than "it's a thing what's in the bible", and this is along those lines. Upvoted.

One nitpick:

Amnestiacs

I don't think I've seen the compromise before — well done — but they're "amnestics".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beautiful. I thoroughly enjoyed this. My personal preference is for SCPs that don't kill people, but do… unexpected things. This was most unexpected.

I especially like the use of a theological concept relatively unfamiliar to most non-Muslims.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you- I will fix that now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really enjoyed this.
One minor error - in the second paragraph of 3941-3's description you refer to him as simply SCP-3941.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant. This is seriously good shit for a first SCP, enjoy the tone this sets and the resolution of the plot at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• SCP 3941-3 is conversant in Farsi, Iraqi Arabic, Classical Arabic, English and French. SCP-3941-3 will respond to its official designation, but will refuse to answer questions if the interviewer insinuates that he is not the Twelfth Imam, as he claims. He has shown a keen interest in theological debate, apparently being relatively well-versed in a variety of religious traditions beyond his own. SCP-3941 seems to enjoy speaking to Foundation personnel; he has spoken positively of conversations with Researcher F██████ on several occasions.

Only those seven lines already makes my upvote worth it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Top tier. Engaging, immersive and has a good cohesive vibe going. 10/10, 11/10 with rice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image is from here, and is under a creative commons license: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Double_knock_old_door_(2624799098).jpg

Many thanks to Wogglebug for their feedback.

If there are any horrific inaccuracies about Shi'ism in this article, I would be very pleased to hear them in order to rectify any mistakes.
